observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed that henceforth, the Municipal Development Council submit during the annual investment programming their listing of priority projects covering a period of three years for deliberation and eventual inclusion in the Local Development Investment Program, of which shall be the source of the projects or programs identified in the Annual Investment Plan of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Management has sent out instructions to all members of the Municipal Development Council for the submission of priority projects for the next planning period.
observations.reason: Management committed to conduct listing of priority projects and from the lists are priority projects that will be included in the LDIP in their next planning activity.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to transfer the recognition and recording of the completed wash facilities to the Department of Education having control and use over the asset; and for the Municipal Accountant to prepare an adjusting entry to effect the transfer, for fair presentation of the affected accounts in the financial statements.

We have recommended and Management agreed to increase the Municipal Treasurer’s fidelity bond that is sufficient to cover his accountabilities to ensure the recovery of loss in cases of defalcations, shortages and unrelieved losses.
We have recommended further and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to hereon ensure that future cash advances are within his approved maximum cash accountabilities.

We have recommended and Management agreed to update its Revenue Code to improve the revenue generation of the Municipality, thereby, more funds will be available in the attainment of the Municipality’s Programs, Projects and Activities, pursuant to Section 191 of R.A. No. 7160.

We have recommended and Management agreed to create a guideline for the grant of Assistance to Individuals in Crisis Situation (AICS) which enumerates the criteria and selection process of qualified beneficiaries and specific rates for its assistance that may be given to beneficiaries.

We have recommended that management require the contractors to submit the approved construction safety and health program as required under Section 37.2.3(f) of the RIRR of R.A. No. 9184, otherwise such transactions shall be disallowed in audit.

We have recommended that the Municipal School Board hereon prepare the Special Education Fund Budget based on the DepEd approved School Investment Plan and the Division Education Development Plan pursuant to the provisions of the DepEd, DBM, & DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 January 19, 2017.

We have recommended and Management agreed to:
compel the contractors to expedite the completion of the aforementioned projects to shorten the gap of incurred delays, if not to complete on time, or
consider terminating the contracts if the contractors do not heed to the demands of the Municipality in expediting the completion of the projects, and to exercise its option of taking over or to negotiate the contract to other willing contractors.

We have recommended and Management agreed to require the contractor concerned to immediately carry out the necessary corrective measures to address the noted defects or deficiencies to prevent further deterioration and early destruction of the projects.
We have recommended and Management agreed to require the persons determined to be liable to settle the audit disallowances in accordance with Section 10 of COA Circular No. 2009-006.

We have recommended and Management agreed to include a clause in the purchase order making reference to the general and specific conditions of the contract pursuant to the provisions of the Procurement Manual for LGUs, Volume II; and

Henceforth, Management agreed to notify the Office of the Auditor within 24 hours after acceptance of deliveries pursuant to the provision of COA Circular No. 95-006.
observations.managementAction:
Management updated the Purchase Order forms to include a clause making reference to the general and specific conditions of the contract.

Further, notices were sent to COA within the required time regarding deliveries.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended and Management agreed to issue a demand letter requiring the official concerned to liquidate his cash advance, otherwise withhold any amount due him.



Henceforth, Management agreed to ensure compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 regarding the granting, utilization, and liquidation of cash advances, as follows:

that cash advances for local travels be liquidated within 30 days upon return of the official or employee to his permanent official station; and 

that all cash advances shall be liquidated at the end of the year.
observations.managementAction:
Management sent a reminder letter to the accountable officer, however, the amount of ₱25,711.00 remained unliquidated at the end of the year. 

All cash advance during the year were liquidated within 30 days upon return of an employee or official and that only the CA on prior years remained unliquidated at year end.
observations.reason: Settled on February 21, 2022 with the presentation of liquidation documents and payment of ₱12,000.00 representing unliquidated expenses.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to conduct an annual inventory taking for all the property owned/controlled by the Municipality; and to prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property and Equipment in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-006.
observations.managementAction: A Report on the conducted Physical Count of PPEs for the periods ending June 30, 2021 and December 31, 2021 were submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended and Management to review their forecasting methods, to make projections realistic, achievable or attainable and to ensure that all accounts are included taking prior years’ experience and statistics as aids in setting targets or projections.

Further, we have recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer prepare the Quarterly Report of Income (LBAc Form No. 1) on periodic basis for the Finance Committee to monitor the status of revenue generation and at the same time adopt or institute measures that will likely improve collection for accounts that missed their targets so that all plans and programs are assured of funding and implementation.
observations.managementAction: Quarterly Report of Income (LBAc Form No. 1) had been prepared and were carefully reviewed and were used as part basis in coming up revenue projections.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to prepare and submit to the COA Auditor the Monthly Reports of Fuel Consumption and Official Travels, for evaluation and verification purposes, pursuant to COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 on the rules and regulations governing the use of government motor vehicles and fuel consumption.
observations.managementAction: Monthly reports on fuel consumption are prepared and being submitted to the Office of the COA auditor.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to ensure that DTTs are properly and completely accomplish before a vehicle can depart from the garage/motorpool, as provided for in Annex A of COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977.
observations.managementAction: The driver's trip ticket were properly accomplished as this is needed in the preparation of the monthly report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to tag all LGU properties and equipment with property stickers for proper identification and henceforth, do the same for succeeding issuances pursuant to Section 122(m) of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I, and the guidelines set forth in Chapter V of the Training Handbook on Property Supply Management System.
observations.reason: However,  as of year end PPEs were not yet tagged with property stickers for proper identification.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to prioritize the conduct of mandatory capacity building program on GAD for the members of the GFPS Executive Committee and TWG, including elected local officials and department heads, pursuant to the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: Management were not able to send concerned officials for seminar due to COVID19 restrictions. Only two were accommodated during a seminar sponsored by the provincial government.
observations.reason: Only two officials were able to attend a GAD sponsored seminar during the year.
